ogTitle: Herne Hill vs Hesket: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Herne Hill and Hesket.
heading: Herne Hill vs Hesket — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Herne Hill, Victoria and Hesket, Victoria. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Herne Hill has a lower overall climate risk score (34/100) compared to Hesket (37/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Herne Hill or Hesket safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Herne Hill has a flood risk score of 53/100, while Hesket scores 50/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Herne Hill scores 35/100 for bushfire risk. Hesket scores 61/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Herne Hill or Hesket?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Herne Hill has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.03% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
